pullRequest.title: Call `get_export_fields()` (not `get_import_fields()`) to populate admin UI export form
pullRequest.body:
## Fix

This commit closes #2094 where export forms in the admin interface were incorrectly displaying import fields instead of export fields.

## Changes Made

### Core Fix
- Added `get_user_visible_import_fields()` method to return fields for import interface
- Added `get_user_visible_export_fields()` method to return fields for export interface
- Updated admin.py to use context-specific methods:
  - Import contexts now use `get_user_visible_import_fields()`
  - Export contexts now use `get_user_visible_export_fields()`

### Deprecation
- Deprecated `get_user_visible_fields()` in v4.4 for removal in v6.0
- Added clear deprecation warning with migration guidance
- Method now issues DeprecationWarning directing users to new methods

### Tests
- Added comprehensive tests for import/export field display in admin forms
- Added test coverage for deprecation warning
- All existing tests continue to pass

## Breaking Changes

Yes - export now calls `get_user_visible_export_fields()` meaning that `get_import_fields()` is no longer used to populate export fields.  If any existing implementations are relying on the previous behaviour then they will break.
This is why the fix should be released in v5 (with deprecation of `get_user_visible_fields()`).

## Migration Path
Replace calls to:
- `get_user_visible_fields()` in import contexts with `get_user_visible_import_fields()`
- `get_user_visible_fields()` in export contexts with `get_user_visible_export_fields()`

pullRequest.title: Fix Admin integration form name collision
pullRequest.body:
**Problem**

Closes #2106 

**Solution**

Prepended unique identifier string to form elements which are for django-import-export only.  This means that there is very little chance of a name collision during export with any fields present on the model.

Note that because this has the potential to break existing implementations we should probably release in v5.

It will break existing code if users have customized form submissions and they are still relying on form elements to be called 'resource', 'format' etc.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Ran tests
- Manual testing

**Note**

- In theory all form fields should be protected by the prefix, however this only affects 'export' form fields because they can be disrupted by field names in the model.

pullRequest.title: Fix/foreign key widget export issue
pullRequest.body:
**Problem**

Closes #2107.

This is a regression from v3.  When a Resource uses the `widget` syntax to declare the field for export, the declared field is ignored and the Resource id is exported.

Stems for changes in #1717.  This was a performance fix for slow exports, but it had a knock-on effect where exports with `ForeignKeyWidget` were affected (#1824).

This is a similar issue, but for cases when the export is defined using the `widget` declaration:

```
        widgets = {
            "author": {"field": "name"},
        }
```

**Solution**

Extended the existing check for declared ForeignKeyWidget.  The `key_is_id` optimization is only applied if there is no custom `widgets` declaration.

**Acceptance Criteria**

- Added tests

pullRequest.title: Fix console error 'resource select input not found' on export
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #2120
- When only one resource is configured, `_init_resources()` replaces the `Select` widget with `HiddenInput()` but drops the `id="id_resource"` attribute
- `export_selectable_fields.js` then fails to find `#id_resource` and logs a console error
- Fix: preserve the `id` attribute on the replacement `HiddenInput` widget

## Test plan
- [x] Verify `make test` passes (64 form/export tests confirmed passing)
- [x] Navigate to admin changelist, select items, choose "Export selected" action, confirm no console error

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: do not cast falsy values to None / null
pullRequest.body:
valid json values that cannot be rendered properly without this fix:

- 0
- ""
- False
- []
- {}

**Problem**

https://github.com/django-import-export/django-import-export/issues/2122

**Solution**

only yield `None` as `null`

pullRequest.title: Fix IncorrectLookupParameters when exporting from filtered change view
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- When navigating to an admin change view from a filtered changelist, Django adds `_changelist_filters` to the URL. Clicking Export passes this parameter to `ChangeList`, which tries to resolve it as a model field lookup, raising `IncorrectLookupParameters`.
- Fix: override `get_filters_params()` in `ExportChangeList` to strip `_changelist_filters`, since it is not a queryset filter — it's only used by Django to preserve filter state for the "Back to list" link.
- Added test that reproduces the exact scenario.

## Test plan
- [x] New test `test_get_export_queryset_with_changelist_filters_param` passes
- [x] All 51 export tests pass
- [x] Manual verification: navigate to a change view from a filtered list, click Export, confirm no exception

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
